metadata.title: PAPER2WEB: Let’s Make Your Paper Alive!
metadata.description: An overview of PAPER2WEB, a benchmark and framework for transforming academic papers into interactive, multimedia-rich websites using PWAGENT.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content:
Yuhang Chen, Tianpeng Lv, Siyi Zhang, Yixiang Yin, Yao Wan, Philip S. Yu, Dongping Chen

Academic papers in PDF format lack interactivity and multimedia support, limiting effective dissemination. PAPER2WEB addresses this by introducing a unified platform (Paper2All) that converts papers into interactive websites, integrating text, visuals, and dynamic elements for broader accessibility and engagement.

sections.title: Challenges in Current Academic Webpage Generation
sections.content: Existing methods like arXiv HTML and alphaXiv suffer from distorted layouts, inconsistent scaling, detached captions, missing responsiveness, and limited interactivity. Direct LLM-based generation struggles with long contexts and robust multimedia integration, resulting in static or disordered presentations that hinder user experience.

sections.title: PAPER2WEB Dataset and Evaluation Framework
sections.content: PAPER2WEB introduces a large-scale dataset of 10,716 AI papers paired with human-created project homepages, collected from top conferences (2020–2025). The evaluation suite includes: (1) Connectivity & Completeness (measuring link quality and structural fidelity with image-text balance and information efficiency priors), (2) Human/MLLM-as-a-Judge (scoring interactivity, aesthetics, and informativeness on a 1–5 scale), and (3) PaperQuiz (assessing knowledge retention via verbatim and interpretive QA with a verbosity penalty).

sections.title: PWAGENT: Autonomous Pipeline for Webpage Generation
sections.content: PWAGENT is a multi-agent framework that transforms papers into interactive websites through three stages: (1) Paper Decomposition—extracting structured textual, visual, and link assets; (2) MCP Ingestion—storing assets in a semantically aligned repository with layout budgets; and (3) Agent-driven Iterative Refinement—using an MLLM orchestrator to inspect rendered views and issue targeted edits for visual balance, hierarchy, and multimedia anchoring.

sections.title: Experimental Results and Comparisons
sections.content: PWAGENT outperforms all baselines across metrics. It achieves the highest scores in Connectivity, Completeness, and Holistic Evaluation (Interactivity, Aesthetics, Informativeness). On PaperQuiz, it maintains high raw scores while incurring minimal verbosity penalties, demonstrating effective context compression. Cost analysis shows PWAGENT uses only ~$0.012 per page, significantly cheaper than end-to-end LLMs (e.g., GPT-4o at ~$0.141). Qualitative comparisons confirm PWAGENT generates rich, balanced, and interactive layouts surpassing arXiv, alphaXiv, and template-based methods.
